skill.name: agent-tools
skill.description: Run 150+ AI apps via inference.sh CLI - image generation, video creation, LLMs, search, 3D, Twitter automation. Models: FLUX, Veo, Gemini, Grok, Claude, Seedance, OmniHuman, Tavily, Exa, OpenRouter, and many more.
skill.summary: Documentation for running 150+ AI models via inference.sh CLI including image generation, video creation, LLMs, search, and automation tools.
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 203 patterns in documentation files, but all are false positives. The skill consists of Markdown documentation with code examples, not executable code. Backticks are markdown formatting, URLs are documentation links, and environment variable references are standard API authentication documentation. The documented curl|sh install pattern is industry-standard for CLI tools. No security concerns for publication.

content.user_title: Run 150+ AI Apps via inference.sh CLI
content.value_statement: Access 150+ AI models for image generation, video creation, LLMs, and search through a unified CLI interface. No GPU or API key management required - inference.sh handles cloud execution.
actual_capabilities: Execute 150+ AI apps including FLUX, Veo, Gemini, Grok, and Claude via CLI commands
Generate images, videos, 3D models, and audio using cloud-based AI models
Upload local files for image-to-video, upscaling, and avatar generation
Perform web search using Tavily and Exa search engines
Automate Twitter/X actions including posting tweets and sending DMs
Manage asynchronous tasks with status tracking for long-running operations
limitations: Requires inference.sh account and API authentication to execute commands
Cloud-based execution means files must be uploaded for processing
Subject to inference.sh quota and credit limits for paid models
Cannot run local AI models - requires internet connection

use_cases.title: AI Content Creation
use_cases.description: Generate marketing images with FLUX, create product videos with Veo, or produce 3D models for e-commerce without local GPU hardware.

use_cases.title: LLM Integration
use_cases.description: Call Claude, Gemini, or other LLMs through a unified interface for chat, analysis, and content generation tasks.

use_cases.title: Research and Search
use_cases.description: Use Tavily and Exa for AI-enhanced web search, extract webpage content, and gather research data programmatically.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image using FLUX with the prompt: a cat astronaut floating in space
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick image generation for presentations or social media

prompt_templates.title: Video Creation from Text
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a video using Veo 3.1 with the prompt: drone shot flying over a forest at sunset
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating B-roll or intro videos for content

prompt_templates.title: Local File Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Upscale the image at /path/to/photo.jpg by 2x using Topaz upscaler
prompt_templates.scenario: Enhancing local images without manual upload steps

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Step Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate sample input for google/veo-3-1-fast, save to input.json, then run with the edited prompt and track the task status
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up and executing complex AI workflows

output_examples.input: infsh app run falai/flux-dev-lora --input '{"prompt": "a cat astronaut"}'
output_examples.output: Task completed with generated image URL: https://cloud.inference.sh/generated/image-123.png

output_examples.input: infsh app run tavily/search-assistant --input '{"query": "latest AI news"}'
output_examples.output: Search results returned with 5 relevant articles including titles, URLs, and summaries
Task status object with state, created_at, completed_at, and output containing result URLs

best_practices: Use --no-wait flag for long-running video generation tasks and poll status with task get
Generate sample input files with app sample command to understand required input schema
Upload local files by providing file paths directly - CLI handles upload automatically
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode API keys in scripts - use environment variables or infsh login
Avoid running apps without checking input schema first with app get
Do not ignore task errors - always check task status for failures

faq.question: How do I install the inference.sh CLI?
faq.answer: Run curl -fsSL https://cli.inference.sh | sh then run infsh login to authenticate. The installer downloads the correct binary for your OS and architecture.

faq.question: Do I need a GPU to run AI models?
faq.answer: No, all models run on inference.sh cloud infrastructure. Your local machine only needs the CLI to submit jobs and receive results.

faq.question: How does authentication work?
faq.answer: Run infsh login for interactive authentication or set INFSH_API_KEY environment variable for CI/CD and automated scripts.

faq.question: Can I use local files with cloud models?
faq.answer: Yes, provide local file paths in the input JSON and the CLI automatically uploads files before running the app.

faq.question: What happens if a task takes too long?
faq.answer: Use --no-wait flag to submit and return immediately, then poll task status with infsh task get <task-id>.

faq.question: How do I find available AI models?
faq.answer: Use infsh app list to see all apps, infsh app list --category image for specific categories, or infsh app search flux to search.
